2604.21764.title: Thinking with Reasoning Skills: Fewer Tokens, More Accuracy
2604.21764.paper_summary: Reasoning LLMs often spend substantial tokens on long intermediate reasoning traces (e.g., chain-of-thought) when solving new problems. We propose to summarize and store reusable reasoning skills distilled from extensive deliberation and trial-and-error exploration, and to retrieve these skills at inference time to guide future reasoning. Unlike the prevailing \emph{reasoning from scratch} paradigm, our approach first recalls relevant skills for each query, helping the model avoid redundant detours and focus on effective solution paths. We evaluate our method on coding and mathematical reasoning tasks, and find that it significantly reduces reasoning tokens while improving overall performance. The resulting lower per-request cost indicates strong practical and economic potential for real-world deployment.
2604.21764.paper_summary_zh: 推理大型語言模型（LLMs）在解決新問題時，經常會在長期的中間推理過程（例如，思考鏈）上消耗大量的標記。 我們提出從廣泛的深思熟慮和反覆試驗探索中提煉出可重用的推理技能，並在推理時檢索這些技能以指導未來的推理。 與現行的\emph{從零開始推理}範式不同，我們的方法首先為每個查詢回憶相關技能，幫助模型避免冗餘的迂迴，並專注於有效的解決路徑。 我們在編碼和數學推理任務上評估我們的方法，發現它顯著減少了推理標記，同時改善了整體性能。 由此產生的每次請求成本降低，顯示出在現實世界部署中的強大實用性和經濟潛力。
2604.21764.author: Guangxiang Zhao et.al.
2604.21764.authors: Guangxiang Zhao, Qilong Shi, Xusen Xiao, Xiangzheng Zhang, Tong Yang, Lin Sun
